mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuation multiples and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in the unemployment rate below 8.0 percent signals labor market normalization, which boosts consumer spending expectations and drives equity valuations higher over the next month.
failure_condition: The signal produces negative or flat 20-day forward returns in more than 55% of triggered instances over a 5-year out-of-sample test.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth exceeding 2.0 percent annually while core inflation remains below 2.5 percent creates a disinflationary expansion environment that maximizes corporate profit margins and equity risk premiums.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform a buy-and-hold benchmark by at least 1.5% over any 3-year rolling window, or the interaction term coefficient becomes statistically insignificant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: A monthly industrial production index reading above 95.0 indicates robust manufacturing capacity utilization, which directly translates to higher corporate revenues and positive equity returns over the following week.
failure_condition: Forward 5-day returns average below zero when INDPRO exceeds the threshold for three consecutive months, indicating supply-side exhaustion rather than demand-driven gains.

mechanism_narrative: A continuous month-over-month increase in real retail and food services sales directly measures consumer demand strength, which reliably predicts positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: The rolling 12-month correlation between RSAFS month-over-month changes and 20-day forward SPX returns drops below 0.15 for two consecutive years.

mechanism_narrative: A three-month cumulative increase in housing starts exceeding 50.0 thousand units signals a sustained recovery in the construction sector and related supply chains, lifting equity valuations over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The hypothesis is rejected if 60-day forward returns following the trigger are statistically indistinguishable from zero (95% confidence interval includes zero) across three distinct housing cycles.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous nonfarm payroll growth exceeding 150.0 thousand and an unemployment rate decline of at least 0.2 percentage points confirms a self-reinforcing labor market expansion that drives equity risk premiums higher over the next week.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS monthly change exceeds 150.0 and UNRATE monthly change falls below -0.2
failure_condition: The interaction term loses predictive power (t-statistic < 1.5) when tested against a baseline model containing only PAYEMS changes over a 10-year walk-forward period.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate below 0.50 percent combined with a CPI reading above 2.0 percent forces the central bank into a policy constraint that limits equity valuation upside, resulting in negative forward returns over the next month.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS drops below 0.5% and CPIAUCSL rises above 2.0%
failure_condition: The joint signal produces positive 20-day forward returns in more than 60% of historical instances, or the interaction coefficient becomes positive and statistically significant (p < 0.05).
